content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of length NNN.

You must perform the following operation **exactly** KKK times:


Choose two distinct indices iii and jjj (1≤i<j≤N1 \le i \lt j \leq N1≤i<j≤N).
Then,

If (Ai+Aj)(A_i + A_{j})(Ai​+Aj​) is even, append Ai+Aj2\frac{A_i + A_{j}}{2}2Ai​+Aj​​ to AAA.
If (Ai+Aj)(A_i + A_{j})(Ai​+Aj​) is odd, append Ai+Aj+12\frac{A_i + A_{j} + 1}{2}2Ai​+Aj​+1​ to AAA.


NNN is increased by 111 after this.
Find the minimum possible sum of the final array, after performing this operation KKK times.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — the length of the array and the number of times the operation must be performed.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum possible sum of the array after KKK operations.
constraints: The sum of NNN across all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Choose the elements A2=5A_2 = 5A2​=5 and A4=4A_4 = 4A4​=4, to obtain avg(5,4)=5\text{avg}(5, 4) = 5avg(5,4)=5, and append it to AAA.
The array is now [6,5,7,4,7,5][6, 5, 7, 4, 7, 5][6,5,7,4,7,5], with a sum of 343434. This is optimal.
Test case 222: Two moves need to be performed. In both moves, choose A1=1A_1 = 1A1​=1 and A5=1A_5 = 1A5​=1, which give avg(1,1)=1\text{avg}(1, 1) = 1avg(1,1)=1.
The final array will be [1,4,10,8,1,1,1][1, 4, 10, 8, 1, 1, 1][1,4,10,8,1,1,1] with a sum of 262626, which is optimal.
